1.gl: a desert; especially (with the article prefix) the (generally) sterile valley of the Jordan and its continuation to the Red Sea
1.kj: Arabah, champaign, desert, evening, heaven, plain, wilderness. See also H1026 (בֵּית הָעֲרָבָה).
1.dv: from H6150 (עָרַב) (in the sense of sterility);

1.gl: to break forth as a bud, i.e. bloom; generally, to spread; specifically, to fly (as extending the wings); figuratively, to flourish
1.kj: [idiom] abroad, [idiom] abundantly, blossom, break forth (out), bud, flourish, make fly, grow, spread, spring (up).

1.gl: a pasture (i.e. open field, whither cattle are driven); by implication, a desert; also speech (including its organs)
1.dv: from H1696 (דָבַר) in the sense of driving;

1.gl: properly, to spin round (under the influence of any violent emotion), i.e. usually rejoice, or (as cringing) fear
1.kj: be glad, joy, be joyful, rejoice.
1.dv: or (by permutation) גּוּל; a primitive root;

1.kj: barren, drought, dry (land, place), solitary place, wilderness.
1.dv: from an unused root meaning to parch;

1.kj: be glad, [idiom] greatly, joy, make mirth, rejoice.
